facts: Same galaxies imaged by KiDS (deeper, higher-res) and DESI (shallower, lower-res) on GAMA fields.
hypotheses: Morphology models trained on KiDS transfer to DESI imaging of the same galaxies.
Cross-instrument shift on the same galaxies functions as a label-stability test for cross-galaxy generalization.
intuitions: Zoobot 2024-25 and LSST early-release morphology classifications likely cross-check against KiDS/DESI models.
unknowns: Cross-instrument shift on the same galaxies decouples population shift from image-formation shift — the cleanest such pair in the corpus — but there is no established primitive for 'label-stable observation shift'.
